data.title: Full Truck Alliance on positive Catalyst Watch for earnings growth—Citi Research
data.description:
Citi Research added Full Truck Alliance (YMM) to its 30-day Catalyst Watch with a Buy recommendation as the company now shows better earnings growth visibility.

The earnings potential reflects the company’s dominance in a “well-established ecosystem,” a ramp-up in commission revenue that should drive the company’s sales and earnings growth, and penetration in the less-than-truckload and intra-city transportation market that “shows promise,” Citi’s Brian Gong noted.

However, Gong still assigns a “High Risk” rating to the stock given its short trading history and the ongoing data investigation into the company. This investigation could result in material fines and a protracted suspension of new user registrations.

Additionally, a slow ramp-up of commissions and the need to offer large-scale incentives in the intra-city transportation market are also potential risks to the stock’s upside.

data.title: Wall Street slides as hopes of U.S.-Iran peace deal fade
data.description:
Wall Street traded lower on Monday as optimism over a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal faded, with escalating tensions in the Middle East weighing on investor sentiment and prompting a pullback in equities following last week’s rally.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) slipped -0.2%, while the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) dipped -0.1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) hovered near even. 

On a sector-by-sector basis, six of the 11 S&P segments are trading up in the green, with energy at the top as oil (CL1:COM) was up over 4%. At the other end of the spectrum, consumer discretionary has suffered the most so far in the session. 

Iran will not participate in a second round of talks with the United States, according to Iranian state media, which said Tehran objected to what it described as excessive and unrealistic demands from Washington.

U.S. Treasury yields were slightly higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.25%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) climbed 2 basis points to 3.72%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) was mostly unchanged at 4.88%. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Marvell Technology (MRVL) climbed +4.9%, while shares of Intel (INTC) dropped -3.2%.

data.title: Strategy acquires 34K BTC using an ATM-funded $2.54B raise
data.description:
Strategy (MSTR) announced on Monday that it has acquired 34.16K Bitcoin (BTC-USD) for ~$2.54B at an average price of ~$74.4K per bitcoin (BTC-USD), according to its SEC Form 8-K filing.

The purchase came right after it raised capital through its ATM program between April 13 and 19, maintaining a total issuance capacity of ~$26.7B and raising ~$366M via a mix of preferred stocks STRK, STRD, and common stock.

This structure clearly shows that capital raised from the market was directly converted into Bitcoin (BTC-USD). As shared by Michael Saylor, Strategy (MSTR) now holds 815.06K BTC acquired for ~$61.56B at an average price of ~$75.5K per bitcoin (BTC-USD) as of April 19, 2026.

The company also reported a BTC yield of 9.5% YTD 2026, showing its aggressive accumulation strategy. Moreover, as reported in the filing, all purchase prices include fees and expenses.

Strategy (MSTR) is trading ~1.75% lower to ~$163.21, while Bitcoin (BTC-USD), on the other hand, is trading ~1.02% down to ~$75K.

data.title: Southern Copper appoints interim CEO; Peru reauthorizes Tia Maria project
data.description:
Southern Copper (SCCO) disclosed late Friday it appointed board member Leonardo Contreras Lerdo de Tejada as interim CEO, following the unexpected passing of President and CEO Oscar Gonzalez Rocha.

The company said its board is conducting a search for a permanent CEO as part of its internal succession planning process.

Separately, Peru's government said it reauthorized the mining permit for Southern Copper’s (SCCO) $1.8B Tia Maria project, after authorities had forced a fresh review of the project.

Peru's Ministry of Energy and Mines confirmed the project has met the necessary regulatory requirements, including environmental certification, ownership, and safety and health obligations.

Tia Maria is expected to begin production by the year-end 2026 or early 2027, delivering 120K metric tons/year of copper over a 20-year lifespan.

data.title: JD Vance, U.S. delegation head to Pakistan for another round of talks - reports
data.description:
Vice President JD Vance, along with the U.S. delegation, is reportedly expected to land in Islamabad, Pakistan, within hours.  

President Donald Trump said he was willing to meet senior Iranian leaders if the talks materialize properly, the New York Post reported. 

“We’re supposed to have the talks… So I would assume at this point nobody’s playing games,” Trump said in a brief interview with the Post. 

Earlier, Iran had said it would not participate in a second round of talks with the United States, according to Iranian state media, which said Tehran objected to what it described as excessive and unrealistic demands from Washington.

Despite Tehran’s announcement, Trump had said U.S. negotiators were prepared to continue talks and planned to travel to Pakistan for another round of meetings.

The diplomatic setback comes as investors monitor disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for global oil shipments. Any prolonged impasse between Washington and Tehran could intensify concerns over energy supplies and regional stability.

“Well, I don’t want to get into that with you,” he told the Post when asked if the U.S. would escalate matters if a deal is not reached. “You can imagine. It wouldn’t be pretty,” he had added.

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Central banks may face ‘tyrannical position’ amid Iran war, Mizuho analyst warns
data.description:
Central banks across Asia could be forced to raise interest rates despite weakening economies because the war in the Middle East is putting intense pressure on currencies, according to Vishnu Varathan of Mizuho Bank.

In an interview with CNBC on Monday, Varathan said monetary policymakers may find themselves in a “tyrannical position” where they cannot provide economic support and must instead prioritize currency stability—even at the cost of short-term harm to growth.

“They know from the AFC experience that you can’t let currency stability get away. It’s hard to regain it,” Varathan said, referring to the Asian Financial Crisis of the late 1990s. “So they need to probably hike despite the economy hurting.”

Varathan cautioned investors against premature optimism when oil (USO) (BNO) prices temporarily decline, noting that screen prices do not reflect actual delivery costs. He said access to refined products has become increasingly challenging across Asia (AIA) (EEMA), creating persistent energy and food supply challenges.

“We can’t get prematurely euphoric about any deal signed because the lingering adverse effects mean that we don’t get out of this quickly,” he said.

The analyst also warned of deepening inequality, saying the conflict would push economies “further down the K-shape,” making policy responses increasingly difficult to implement effectively.

data.title: Nasdaq Dividend Preview: 13-year growth streak expected to continue with April hike
Blue Owl Capital Inc. filing confirms co-CEOs no longer backing personal loans with company shares
data.description:
Filings made by Blue Owl Capital  Inc. (OWL) after Friday's close confirmed that co-CEOs Doug Ostrover and Marc Lipschultz adjusted the terms of their personal loans so that the company's shares are no longer pledged as collateral. The Wall Street Journal had previously reported the revised loan arrangements, citing people familiar with the matter.

The alternative asset manager's latest proxy statement said none of its directors or executive officers currently had company stock pledged under loan arrangements. In previous filings, the company said the Ostrover had put up about 43M shares and Lipschultz about 33M shares for collateral against personal loans.

With that much at stake, Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL) had previously said that its shares "could decline materially" if the two executives had to sell stock to meet a margin call, Bloomberg News reported.

In the past year, Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL) shares fell 43% amid fears that private credit investments in software faced challenges from the proliferation of AI.

In early trading on Monday, Blue Owl Capital Inc. (OWL) shares slipped 0.6% after rising 2.1% on Friday.

data.title: PVH is the most overbought consumer discretionary stock as Q1 earnings roll on
data.description:
As the market moves into the second week of Q1 earnings, we look at the most overbought stocks in the consumer discretionary sector.

Below is a list of the top 10 consumer discretionary stocks ranked based on their RSI. The list includes primary ticker stocks within the sector, featuring companies with market capitalizations ranging from approximately $2.6 billion to over $2.6 trillion.

The list is topped by PVH Corp. (PVH), with an RSI of 81. Crocs (CROX) and Amazon (AMZN) follow closely behind, while Stride, Inc. (LRN) and V.F. Corporation (VFC) round out the top five, both showing an RSI of 76. All ten companies on the list, including Harley-Davidson (HOG), eBay (EBAY), and DICK’S Sporting Goods (DKS), currently maintain RSI values of 73 or higher.

The Relative Strength Index is a momentum oscillator that measures the velocity and magnitude of price changes, plotting them on a scale from 0 to 100. RSI compares the magnitude of recent gains to recent losses over a chosen lookback period, typically 14 days.

Here is the list:

  1. PVH Corp. (PVH), RSI: 81

  2. Crocs, Inc. (CROX), RSI: 78

  3. Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN), RSI: 77

  4. Stride, Inc. (LRN), RSI: 76

  5. V.F. Corporation (VFC), RSI: 76

  6. Tri Pointe Homes, Inc. (TPH), RSI: 75

  7. Harley-Davidson, Inc. (HOG), RSI: 74

  8. eBay Inc. (EBAY), RSI: 74

  9. Choice Hotels International, Inc. (CHH), RSI: 74

  10. DICK’S Sporting Goods, Inc. (DKS), RSI: 73

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI)
